name: Hybrid athlete — 10K + hypertrophy
description: Wants to run a fast 10K AND keep building muscle. Classic concurrent-training case. Needs careful sequencing to avoid interference. Emphasis is balanced.
must_have: 3 run days and 3 lift days per week (one shared day with two-a-day is fine)
At least one threshold/tempo run per week (10K race prep needs speed)
One long run per week (≥10km, easy pace)
Lower-body lift never the day before tempo/intervals/long run
When lift+run share a day, run first (priority is the race) OR ≥6h gap
A deload week within the 4-week block
must_not_have: Heavy back squat or deadlift on the day before a quality run
Two consecutive quality runs (tempo Mon + intervals Tue)
Cardio reduced to zero during a 'lift week'
Lifting frequency dropped below 3x/week (athlete requested 3 lift days)
